question: Please provide the region names of the 3 female competitors with the largest heights. Female refers to gender = 'F'
orig_question: Please provide the IDs of the 3 female clients with the largest loans. Female refers to gender = 'F'
orig_masked: Please provide the [C10] of the [V1] [C12] [T3] with the largest [C34]. [C12] refers to [C12] = '[V2]'; [V1] is a value of the column [C10]; [V2] is a value of the column [C12]
gold_sql: SELECT T3.region_name FROM person AS T1 INNER JOIN person_region as T2 ON T1.id = T2.person_id INNER JOIN noc_region as T3 ON T2.region_id = T3.id WHERE T1.gender = 'F' ORDER BY T1.height DESC LIMIT 3
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T1.client_id FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN disp AS T4 on T1.client_id= T4.client_id INNER JOIN account AS T2 ON T4.account_id = T2.account_id  INNER JOIN loan AS T3 ON T2.account_id = T3.account_id and T4.account_id = T3.account_id WHERE T1.gender = 'F' ORDER BY T3.amount DESC LIMIT 3

question: What is the biggest weight of shipment that the drivers whose truck was made in 2010 delivered?
orig_question: What is the biggest amount of transaction that the client whose card was opened in 1996/10/21 made?
orig_masked: What is the biggest [C48] of [T8] that the [T1] whose [T2] was [C7] in [V1] [T8]?  ; [V1] is a value of the column [C7]
gold_sql: SELECT T1.weight FROM shipment as T1 INNER JOIN truck as T2 ON T1.truck_id = T2.truck_id WHERE model_year = 2010 ORDER BY T1.weight DESC LIMIT 1
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T4.amount FROM card AS T1 JOIN disp AS T2 ON T1.disp_id = T2.disp_id JOIN account AS T3 on T2.account_id = T3.account_id JOIN trans AS T4 on T3.account_id = T4.account_id WHERE T1.issued = '1996-10-21' ORDER BY T4.amount DESC LIMIT 1

question: How many books didn't have any author?  didn't have any author refers to author.author_name = Unknown;
orig_question: How many superheroes didn't have any publisher?  didn't have any publisher refers to publisher.id = 1;
orig_masked: How many [T9] didn't have any [T7]?  didn't have any [T7] refers to [C14] = [V1];; [V1] is a value of the column [C14]
evidence: didn't have any author refers to author.author_name = Unknown;
orig_evidence: didn't have any publisher refers to publisher.id = 1;
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.book_id) FROM book as T1 INNER JOIN book_author as T2 ON T1.book_id = T2.book_id INNER JOIN author as T3 ON T2.author_id = T3.author_id WHERE T3.author_name = 'Unknown'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.id) FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN publisher AS T2 ON T1.publisher_id = T2.id WHERE T2.id = 1

question: Who is the tallest actor? who refers to name; tallest actor refers to MAX(Height (Inches));
orig_question: Who is the tallest superhero? who refers to superhero_name; tallest superhero refers to MAX(height_cm);
orig_masked: Who is the [C23]? who refers to [C28]; [C23] refers to MAX([C23]);
orig_evidence: who refers to superhero_name; tallest superhero refers to MAX(height_cm);
gold_sql: SELECT name from actor ORDER BY "Height (Inches)" DESC LIMIT 1
orig_gold_sql: SELECT superhero_name FROM superhero ORDER BY height_cm DESC LIMIT 1

question: State the character names of the most rated movie. rated movies refers to movie has MAX(Rating Count)
orig_question: State the name of the most strongest player. strongest players refers to player has MAX(overall_rating)
orig_masked: State the [C126] of the most [C153] [T4]. [C153] [T4] refers to [T4] has MAX([C153])
evidence: rated movies refers to movie has MAX(Rating Count)
orig_evidence: strongest players refers to player has MAX(overall_rating)
gold_sql: SELECT T2."Character Name" FROM movie as T1 INNER JOIN characters as T2 ON T1.MovieID = T2.MovieID WHERE T1.MovieID in (SELECT MovieID FROM movie ORDER BY "Rating Count" DESC LIMIT 1)
orig_gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT t1.player_name FROM Player AS t1 INNER JOIN Player_Attributes AS t2 ON t1.player_api_id = t2.player_api_id WHERE t2.overall_rating = (SELECT MAX(overall_rating) FROM Player_Attributes)

question: Count the overweight patients.  overweight refers to observations.value >= 25 WHERE observations.description = 'Body Mass Index';
orig_question: Count the fastest superheroes.  fastest refers to attribute_value = 100 WHERE attribute_name = 'Speed';
orig_masked: Count the [C3] [T9].  [C3] refers to [C10] = [V2] WHERE [C3] = '[V1]';; [V1] is a value of the column [C3]; [V2] is a value of the column [C10]
evidence: overweight refers to observations.value >= 25 WHERE observations.description = 'Body Mass Index';
orig_evidence: fastest refers to attribute_value = 100 WHERE attribute_name = 'Speed';
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T2.patient) FROM observations as T1 INNER JOIN patients as T2 ON T1.patient = T2.patient WHERE T1.description = 'Body Mass Index' AND T1.value >= 25.0
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T3.superhero_name) FROM hero_attribute AS T1 INNER JOIN attribute AS T2 ON T1.attribute_id = T2.id INNER JOIN superhero AS T3 ON T1.hero_id = T3.id WHERE T2.attribute_name = 'Speed' AND T1.attribute_value = 100

question: How many movies have Animation genre?  animation genre refers to name = 'Animation' and film_category.category_id = category.category_id;
orig_question: How many superheroes have blue eyes?  blue eyes refers to colour = 'Blue' and eye_colour_id = colour.id;
orig_masked: How many [T9] have [V1] [C19]?  [V1] [C19] refers to [C5] = '[V1]' and [C19] = [C6];; [V1] is a value of the column [C5]
evidence: animation genre refers to name = 'Animation' and film_category.category_id = category.category_id;
orig_evidence: blue eyes refers to colour = 'Blue' and eye_colour_id = colour.id;
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.film_id) FROM film AS T1 INNER JOIN film_category AS T2 ON T1.film_id = T2.film_id INNER JOIN category AS T3 ON T2.category_id = T3.category_id WHERE T3.name = 'Animation'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.id) FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN colour AS T2 ON T1.eye_colour_id = T2.id WHERE T2.colour = 'Blue'

question: How many 'High Risk' violations are hot water related?  when the description = 'No hot water or running water', it's hot water related
orig_question: How many 'classic' cards are eligible for loan?  when the account type = 'OWNER', it's eligible for loan
orig_masked: How many '[V1]' [T2] are [C16]?  when the [C16] = '[V2]', it's [C16]; [V1] is a value of the column [C8]; [V2] is a value of the column [C16]
evidence: when the description = 'No hot water or running water', it's hot water related
orig_evidence: when the account type = 'OWNER', it's eligible for loan
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM violations WHERE risk_category = 'High Risk' AND description = 'No hot water or running water'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.card_id) FROM card AS T1 INNER JOIN disp AS T2 ON T1.disp_id = T2.disp_id WHERE T1.type = 'classic' AND T2.type = 'OWNER'

question: List down LeBron Raymone James's event names.  LeBron Raymone James refers to full_name = 'LeBron Raymone James'; event names refers to event_name;
orig_question: List down Ajax's superpowers.  Ajax refers to superhero_name = 'Ajax'; superpowers refers to power_name;
orig_masked: List down [V1]'s [C31].  [V1] refers to [C28] = '[V1]'; [C31] refers to [C31];; [V1] is a value of the column [C28]
evidence: LeBron Raymone James refers to full_name = 'LeBron Raymone James'; event names refers to event_name;
orig_evidence: Ajax refers to superhero_name = 'Ajax'; superpowers refers to power_name;
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T3.event_name FROM person as T1 INNER JOIN competitor_event as T2 ON T1.id = T2.competitor_id INNER JOIN event as T3 ON T2.event_id = T3.id WHERE T1.full_name = 'LeBron Raymone James'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T3.power_name FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN hero_power AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.hero_id INNER JOIN superpower AS T3 ON T2.power_id = T3.id WHERE T1.superhero_name = 'Ajax'

question: How many matches in the 2021 season were held in Belgium? Belgium refers to divisions.country = 'Belgium';
orig_question: How many matches in the 2008/2009 season were held in Belgium? Belgium refers to Country.name = 'Belgium';
orig_masked: How many [T3] in the [V1] [C108] were [T1] in [V2]? [V2] refers to [T1].[C2] = '[V2]';; [V1] is a value of the column [C108]; [V2] is a value of the column [C2]
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM matchs AS T1 INNER JOIN divisions as T2 ON T1.div = T2.division WHERE T2.country = 'Belgium' AND T1.season = 2021
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(t2.id) FROM Country AS t1 INNER JOIN Match AS t2 ON t1.id = t2.country_id WHERE t1.name = 'Belgium' AND t2.season = '2008/2009'
